question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.hashOffer] Can a taker submit ratifierData where the abi-decoded callbackData field in the offer hash differs from the actual offer.callbackData passed to take(), under the precondition that the offer was signed with specific callbackData but the taker modifies the callbackData in the Offer struct, triggering the call sequence [take(modifiedOffer, ratifierData, ...) -> hashOffer(modifiedOffer) includes keccak256(modifiedCallbackData) -> isLeaf returns false -> require fails with InvalidProof], violating the invariant that the ratifier must bind the exact offer including callbackData, causing scoped impact: taker cannot modify callbackData to redirect funds, but any callbackData mismatch permanently blocks the fill? Proof idea: differential test modifying callbackData and assert hashOffer produces different leaf hash.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's tick is not a multiple of the market's tickSpacing, under the precondition that the market's tickSpacing was decreased after the offer was ratified into the Merkle tree, triggering the call sequence [take() -> require(offer.tick % _marketState.tickSpacing == 0) -> if old tick is not multiple of new spacing -> reverts with TickNotAccessible], violating the invariant that previously valid offers should remain fillable after tick spacing changes, causing scoped impact: all offers in a Merkle tree at ticks not divisible by the new spacing are permanently blocked? Proof idea: decrease tickSpacing and assert offers at non-divisible ticks revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's tick is valid but the settlement fee at the current timeToMaturity exceeds the offer price (offerPrice < settlementFee), under the precondition that the settlement fee was increased after the offer was ratified, triggering the call sequence [take() -> sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee -> underflows in uint256 -> reverts], violating the invariant that settlement fee changes must not retroactively block existing offers, causing scoped impact: all offers in a Merkle tree with price below the new settlement fee are permanently blocked? Proof idea: increase settlement fee and assert take() reverts for low-price offers.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's market has an enterGate that reverts canIncreaseCredit() for the buyer, under the precondition that the gate was added at market creation and the buyer is not whitelisted, triggering the call sequence [take() -> buyerCreditIncrease > 0 -> IEnterGate(enterGate).canIncreaseCredit(buyer) reverts -> take reverts with BuyerGatedFromIncreasingCredit], violating the invariant that gate checks must not permanently freeze valid offers, causing scoped impact: taker's take is blocked by a gate that reverts, temporarily freezing their ability to fill the offer? Proof idea: mock enterGate to revert and assert take() reverts for credit-increasing fills.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's callback reverts or returns a non-CALLBACK_SUCCESS value, under the precondition that the callback contract has a bug or is malicious, triggering the call sequence [take() -> IBuyCallback(buyerCallback).onBuy(...) reverts -> take reverts with WrongBuyCallbackReturnValue], violating the invariant that a callback revert must not permanently freeze the offer, causing scoped impact: all fills of the offer are blocked if the callback is broken, freezing the maker's offer capacity? Proof idea: mock callback to revert and assert take() reverts, then fix callback and assert take() succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's group is bytes32(0) (default group), and a previous setConsumed(bytes32(0), type(uint256).max, maker) call has set consumed[maker][bytes32(0)] to max, under the precondition that the maker's authorized operator called setConsumed to cancel all offers in the default group, triggering the call sequence [setConsumed(bytes32(0), type(uint256).max, maker) -> consumed[maker][bytes32(0)] = max -> take(offer with group=bytes32(0)) -> consumed += units -> require(consumed <= maxUnits) reverts with ConsumedUnits], violating the invariant that setConsumed should only cancel offers when called by the maker or authorized party, causing scoped impact: authorized operator can grief maker by cancelling all default-group offers? Proof idea: unit test authorized operator calling setConsumed(max) and assert all subsequent takes revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's market maturity is exactly block.timestamp + 1 (one second to maturity), and the settlement fee at timeToMaturity=1 uses the 0-day breakpoint interpolation, under the precondition that the 0-day settlement fee is at its maximum (0.000014e18), triggering the call sequence [take() -> timeToMaturity=1 -> settlementFee uses cbp0/cbp1 interpolation -> fee near maximum -> buyerPrice = offerPrice - fee -> if offerPrice < fee -> underflow revert], violating the invariant that near-maturity offers should remain fillable, causing scoped impact: offers near maturity are blocked by high settlement fees, temporarily freezing taker fills? Proof idea: fuzz timeToMaturity in [0, 1 day] and assert settlementFee never exceeds offerPrice for valid ticks.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's maxAssets is set to exactly the dust threshold (1 wei), and the first fill with units=1 produces buyerAssets=0 due to rounding, under the precondition that buyerPrice < WAD, triggering the call sequence [take(units=1) -> buyerAssets=0 -> consumed += 0 -> require(0 <= 1) passes -> buyer gets 1 credit unit -> repeat indefinitely], violating the invariant that maxAssets=1 should limit total fills to 1 asset worth of capacity, causing scoped impact: a dust maxAssets offer can be filled indefinitely due to rounding, griefing the maker? Proof idea: unit test with maxAssets=1 and buyerPrice < WAD and assert consumed never reaches maxAssets.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can a taker submit a proof with proof.length > 20 (exceeding the maximum tree height), under the precondition that the root is ratified and the offer is valid, triggering the call sequence [isRatified() -> decode(ratifierData) -> proof.length=21 -> isLeaf(root, leafHash, leafIndex, proof) -> loop 21 times -> no revert in isLeaf -> but offerTreeTypeHash(21) would revert with TreeTooHigh if called], violating the invariant that proof length should be bounded, causing scoped impact: a taker submitting an oversized proof causes excessive gas consumption in isLeaf, potentially causing out-of-gas griefing? Proof idea: fuzz proof.length up to 256 and assert gas cost and revert behavior.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's market has 128 collateral types (MAX_COLLATERALS) and the seller has all 16 bitmap slots activated, under the precondition that isHealthy() must iterate all 16 activated collaterals, triggering the call sequence [take() -> sellerDebtIncrease > 0 -> isHealthy(market, id, seller) -> iterate 16 collaterals -> 16 oracle calls -> gas cost near block limit -> take reverts with out-of-gas], violating the invariant that health checks must not be gas-exhaustible, causing scoped impact: a seller with 16 activated collaterals cannot take sell offers due to gas exhaustion in isHealthy? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's group budget is shared across multiple markets (same group bytes32, different market ids), and a fill in market A consumes the group budget, blocking fills in market B, under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] is shared globally across all markets, triggering the call sequence [take in market A -> consumed[maker][group] += units -> take in market B -> consumed[maker][group] + units > maxUnits -> reverts with ConsumedUnits], violating the invariant that group budget should be market-specific, causing scoped impact: a fill in one market blocks all fills in other markets sharing the same group, griefing takers in market B? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's market maturity has passed (block.timestamp > maturity) and the offer is a buy offer (credit-increasing for maker), under the precondition that the taker is the seller with existing credit (sellerDebtIncrease == 0), triggering the call sequence [take() -> timeToMaturity = 0 -> sellerDebtIncrease = 0 -> require(block.timestamp <= maturity || sellerDebtIncrease == 0) passes -> take succeeds post-maturity], violating no invariant (post-maturity credit-reducing trades are allowed), causing scoped impact: verify that post-maturity buy offers can still be filled for credit reduction and that the settlement fee at timeToMaturity=0 is correctly applied? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's pendingFee calculation overflows uint128 due to large continuousFee * timeToMaturity * buyerCreditIncrease, under the precondition that continuousFee is at MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE and timeToMaturity is near 100 years and buyerCreditIncrease is near type(uint128).max, triggering the call sequence [take() -> buyerPendingFeeIncrease = toUint128(buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD)) -> overflow in continuousFee * timeToMaturity -> revert with CastOverflow or arithmetic overflow], violating the invariant that pendingFee must not overflow, causing scoped impact: large credit increases near maximum maturity are blocked by overflow, freezing the taker's fill? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's market has a non-zero enterGate that returns false (not reverts) for canIncreaseDebt(), under the precondition that the taker is the seller and sellerDebtIncrease > 0, triggering the call sequence [take() -> IEnterGate(enterGate).canIncreaseDebt(seller) returns false -> require(false) reverts with SellerGatedFromIncreasingDebt], violating the invariant that gate returning false must block debt increase, causing scoped impact: taker-seller is blocked from taking sell offers in a gated market, temporarily freezing their ability to borrow? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's market has rcfThreshold set to 0, and the liquidation RCF check is effectively disabled for all positions, under the precondition that the market was created with rcfThreshold=0, triggering the call sequence [liquidate() -> maxRepaid check -> _position.collateral[i].mulDivDown(...).zeroFloorSub(maxRepaid) < 0 -> always true -> RCF never applies -> full liquidation always allowed], violating the invariant that RCF should protect borrowers from over-liquidation, causing scoped impact: borrowers in markets with rcfThreshold=0 can always be fully liquidated even if partial liquidation would restore health, griefing borrowers? Proof idea: create market with rcfThreshold=0 and assert full liquidation is always allowed regardless of health.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's market has a collateral with lltv=WAD (LLTV_8=1e18), and the health check maxDebt calculation overflows uint256 due to large collateral * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * WAD / WAD, under the precondition that collateral is near type(uint128).max and price is near type(uint256).max / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, triggering the call sequence [isHealthy() -> maxDebt += collateral.mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(WAD, WAD) -> overflow in intermediate multiplication -> revert], violating the invariant that health checks must not overflow, causing scoped impact: borrowers with large collateral positions cannot be health-checked, blocking withdrawals and liquidations? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's market has a collateral bitmap with a bit set at index 127 (the maximum index), and the msb() function using the clz opcode returns 127, under the precondition that the borrower has activated collateral at index 127, triggering the call sequence [isHealthy() -> msb(collateralBitmap) -> clz opcode on uint128 -> returns 127 -> market.collateralParams[127] accessed -> valid], violating no invariant (this is valid), causing scoped impact: verify that msb() correctly handles the maximum collateral index 127 and that collateralParams[127] is correctly accessed?
